title: Xtra Crumby Malaysian Lentil Bowl
description: Umami and crisp Malaysian dish featuring brown lentils, perfect for a light vegetarian lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the lemongrass and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the brown lentils, chicken broth, sambal oelek, and fish sauce. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lentils are tender, about 20 minutes.
While the lentils are cooking, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the toasted coconut flakes and cook, stirring frequently, until crispy and golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Fluff the cooked lentils with a fork and stir in the crispy fried shallots and chopped cilantro.
Serve the lentil mixture hot, topped with additional crispy fried shallots and toasted coconut flakes if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
